segments.caption: The video opens with a person dressed as a substitute teacher, wearing a blue hoodie with 'LUKE' printed on it, a red hoodie pulled over their head, and black-framed glasses, holding papers and addressing a class. They announce an essay assignment about someone else, and a student, wearing a white Hollister t-shirt, enthusiastically agrees to finish it quickly. The scene cuts to a close-up of hands rapidly writing on paper, then to the student proudly declaring 'Mrs. Spayta, I'm done!' The substitute, now with a black hat added to their headwear, allows the class to play on computers. The next day, the student, still in the white t-shirt, excitedly hands in the completed assignment. The substitute, now wearing a black and gray plaid robe over their hoodie, reveals the student misunderstood the assignment—instead of writing about someone else, they wrote about themselves. The student is shocked and dismayed. The substitute then posts the essay on a whiteboard and walks away, leaving the student to react in disbelief. The final shot shows a video editing software interface with a green screen clip of the student being edited, indicating the entire scene was a skit or video production.
